violations.text: Raw animal foods (such as shell eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as taco toppings) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Food items were rearranged during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/12/2026

Mangonada and Tres leches packaged in the food establishment are lacking proper labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/12/2026

The door to the outside, located in the back of the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026

The individual cutting boards in the kitchen prep area are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair on the grill drawer. Refrigerator door gaskets shall be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Rice was observed placed hot in the walk-in cooler in containers greater than four inches deep. PIC moved rice onto a sheet pan to rapidly cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/06/2025

Mangonada and lard packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. New Violation. Correct By: 01/06/2025
